title: Boat Rental in Naples: A 2026 Budget Guide
topic: a budget guide to renting a boat in Naples
html:
Naples offers a dramatic coastline and reliable winds for a day on the Gulf of Naples. You can set off from the Porto del Molosiglio with a small motorboat from EUR 150 for a crew-free half-day. BoatHire24 connects you with skippers and bareboat owners across the region, making it simple to find a vessel that fits your budget.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Naples?

Prices start at EUR 40 per person for a shared group tour and rise to EUR 1,200 for a crewed yacht. The following breakdown shows typical 2026 rates for the Gulf of Naples.

  • Shared day boat tour (about 4 hours, up to 12 guests), from EUR 40 per person.
  • Small motorboat rented bareboat for half a day, from EUR 150 (5 guests).
  • Catamaran full-day charter with skipper, EUR 600, 900 (8, 10 guests, including fuel).
  • Crewed Sunseeker or Azimut for a full day, EUR 800, 1,200 (up to 8 guests).

For the best deals yacht charter in Naples you can compare verified boat listings and filter by budget, size and skipper preference.

Where to set off in Naples

Most charter departures happen from the Port of Naples (Marina Mergellina or Porto del Molosiglio). The smaller port of Pozzuoli offers direct access to the Phlegraean Islands. From Naples you can reach Capri in about 10 nautical miles, Ischia in 12 nm, and Procida in 14 nm. The bays of Sorrento and Positano are also within a 20 nm cruise.

Which boat to rent

  1. Motorboat bareboat, Great for experienced skippers; holds 5, 6 guests; from EUR 150 for 4 hours.
  2. Sailing catamaran with skipper, Spacious and stable for 8, 10 guests; from EUR 600 for a full day.
  3. Crewed luxury motor yacht (Azimut or Sunseeker), Ideal for a premium day out; 6, 8 guests; from EUR 900.
  4. Inshore RIB, Fast and manoeuvrable for exploring coves near the mainland; up to 6 guests; from EUR 180 for 3 hours.

If you prefer a motor charter with a skipper, visit the Salty Sun Charters charter page for a 45-foot Sunseeker available by the day.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Naples?

In Italy you do not need a licence for boats up to 40 horsepower, but for larger engines you must hold a valid Italian or equivalent licence. Many boat owners on BoatHire24 offer skippered charters, so you do not need a licence if you hire a captain. For bareboat rentals you must provide proof of competence; the operator confirms exact requirements when you book.

In July and August the prevailing Mistral wind can pick up after midday, so aim for a morning departure and anchor for lunch in a sheltered bay such as Baia or Procida where you can swim in calmer water.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 40, 1,200 per day
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Typical trip length: 4 hours to full day
  • Maximum guests (bareboat): 5, 8
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Naples

Can you rent a boat in Naples without a skipper?

Yes, you can rent a small motorboat or RIB without a skipper if you hold a valid boating licence for engines over 40 hp.

Is it cheaper to rent a boat in Naples or Sorrento?

Naples prices are generally lower because of higher competition; a half-day motorboat in Naples costs about 150 euros compared to 180, 200 from Sorrento.

What is the best time of year for a boat trip in the Gulf of Naples?

May, June, September and October offer warm weather, fewer crowds and afternoon breezes that make sailing pleasant.

Whether you want to explore Capri on a crewed yacht or handle a small motorboat yourself, BoatHire24 gives you direct access to owners in Naples. If you own a vessel in the Gulf, you can become a host on the platform and start earning income from your boat in 2026.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Naples and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
